main_sections.title: Follow That Dream
main_sections.prose_summary: This section presents an excerpt from a mother's letter to her teenage daughter Ming, taken from the collection 'My Daughter, My Friend' by Irene Chua, dated 19 June 1995. The letter opens with the direct encouragement 'By all means follow that dream,' establishing its tone of warm yet realistic guidance. The mother argues that greatness is distinguished from mediocrity not by dreams alone but by the sustained effort, sacrifice, and financial investment required to pursue them — citing the widely held view that world-class mastery in any field demands at least ten years of singular, intensive pursuit. She outlines the journey of a dream: it begins with passion, grows into conviction, and demands an honest reckoning of costs before one commits fully. The uphill road, she acknowledges, is sustained only by the knowledge that one is doing what one loves and doing the right thing; when stamina falters, the prospect of success provides the necessary momentum. She invokes the example of Academy Award winners who always thank a support network, reminding Ming that even individual achievement rests on community. The mother then introduces a note of measured caution: many people never move beyond wishful thinking, and circumstances — war, poverty, family obligation — have derailed the dreams of entire generations. She references people whose aspirations to attend Raffles College were destroyed by the Japanese invasion of World War II, and others who sacrificed schooling to support siblings financially. Without dampening Ming's enthusiasm, she urges her daughter to weigh the years required and, if the burning conviction still courses through her veins, to act. Finally, the mother reflects on how life itself reshapes one's dreams over time — her own dream of publishing a book evolved over a decade from her youthful ambitions — and expresses the hope that at least one of Ming's dreams will come true, signing off with 'Love, Mum.' The letter is episodic and conversational in structure, blending personal anecdote, rhetorical questions, and direct address to create an intimate and persuasive tone.

reading_for_comprehension.section_name: Reflect and Respond + Reading for Meaning + Check Your Understanding + Critical Reflection

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read the questions given below and share your answers with your classmates and teacher.

question_bank.stem: What is your dream?
Who inspires you to dream?
What qualities should you have in order to fulfil your dream?
Abdul Kalam said, 'Dream is not that you see in sleep, dream is something that does not let you sleep.' Discuss.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: What role can parents and community play to help children achieve their dreams?
Complete the given web chart. Give as many reasons as you can. (Why is it important to follow a dream?)
State whether the following sentences are true or false. Share your answers with your classmates and teacher.

question_bank.stem: Reaching the peak of skill in a field typically demands a focused and intense dedication for about a decade.
The mother believes that significant effort and personal sacrifices are essential for turning aspirations into reality.
The path to achieving the deepest desires has very little difficulty or a few obstacles.
The mother is of the opinion that a person's life goals and hopes can evolve over time.
Having a strong network of individuals can be a hurdle in pursuing one's ambition.
The mother feels that pursuing a major life goal will not involve any financial expense or sacrifice.
For many individuals, their aspirations remain just wishes because they don't move beyond mere daydreaming.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read the extracts given below and answer the questions that follow. Extract 1: 'It starts with a passion for a particular interest, then comes the conviction that it is imperative to realise it...' (full extract from p.235)

question_bank.stem: Complete the analogy with a suitable word from the extract. enthusiasm: passion:: belief: _____________

Choose the correct option: The author says that a realistic assessment of effort, investment and sacrifice is crucial for preventing _____________. A. the need for external support network B. an early abandonment of the dream C. initial excitement from fading over time D. others from questioning one's commitment
options: the need for external support network
an early abandonment of the dream
initial excitement from fading over time

question_bank.stem: Complete the following with the correct option from those given in brackets. The word 'plunge' as used in the extract indicates a _____________ (complete/gradual) involvement in a task.

Complete the sentence with an appropriate reason. The author's emphasis on 'when you are doing what you love best and are doing the right thing' works as a form of intrinsic motivation because _____________.
Mention one motivating factor besides 'prospect of success', that might keep a person on track, despite running out of stamina.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read the extracts given below and answer the questions that follow. Extract 2: 'From my own experience, life itself may change a person's dreams...' (full extract from p.236)

question_bank.stem: Complete the sentence appropriately. The phrase 'life itself may change a person's dreams' suggests that dreams are not static but rather__________. (evolving/dynamic)
What does the author mean by, 'hopes and aspirations are no less than the original dream of younger days'?
Identify the phrase from the extract that indicates a complex and challenging journey.
Complete the sentence with an appropriate reason. The author says, 'people who are participants in your dreamscape would be many more' because________________.
What is the tone of the author in this extract? A. appreciative and celebratory B. excited and cheerful C. optimistic and encouraging D. eager and inquisitive
The letter begins thus, 'By all means follow that dream'. What do you think Ming must have written to her mother about?
How can one attain an international level of skill in any field? Mention any two ways.
What differentiates the mere dreamers from actual achievers?
How does Ming's mother use critical questions and personal anecdotes to persuade Ming and convey her message effectively?
How does Ming's mother balance encouragement with caution in her advice?

In the letter, Ming's mother specifically addresses the challenges people face in pursuing their dreams. Do you think this advice is still relevant in contemporary society? If yes, why? If no, why not?
What 'costs' in terms of effort, sacrifice, and time are you willing or unwilling to invest to pursue your goals?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read the following sentence from the text: 'The dream will take a much longer time to realise, and the people who are participants in your dreamscape would be many more.' The word 'dreamscape' is a compound word where '-scape' means 'a view or scene of'. Now, refer to a dictionary and find out the meaning of the following words: mindscape, seascape, landscape, cityscape. Then fill in the blanks in the following dialogue between two friends with the words given in the box.

question_bank.stem: Sneha: Your Goa pictures were beautiful! That (i) ___________ was dreamy.
Kiran: Thanks! It was way better than our usual (ii) ___________, so peaceful.
Kiran: True, but I'm craving a quiet (iii) ___________. Forests, hills—something real.
Sneha: Yes, nature clears the (iv)___________, doesn't it?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read each sentence carefully and choose the best meaning for the underlined expression. Then, use that expression in a sentence of your own.

question_bank.stem: She had a dream that seemed to burn in her blood, and she wasn't going to give up on it easily. (i) make her angry (ii) have a passionate desire (iii) cause physical pain (iv) become violent
Preparing for the final exam without proper notes felt like an uphill task. (i) a tough challenge (ii) a slow progress (iii) a hard routine (iv) a difficult choice
The team was buoyed up by their recent victory and felt confident about the next game. (i) energised by success (ii) encouraged by the result (iii) motivated to improve (iv) lifted in spirit

He thought he could ace the exam without studying, but that was just wishful thinking. (i) a clever shortcut but no real effort behind it (ii) a confident guess but lacking proper knowledge (iii) a hopeful belief but unlikely to be true (iv) a strong desire but based on facts and planning
options: a clever shortcut but no real effort behind it
a confident guess but lacking proper knowledge
a hopeful belief but unlikely to be true
a strong desire but based on facts and planning

question_bank.stem: Don't be a wet blanket—we're trying to have fun! (i) spoil-sport (ii) latecomer (iii) rule-breaker (iv) daydreamer
Excitement was coursing through her veins as she stepped onto the stage for the first time. (i) hiding deep inside (ii) flowing through her body (iii) lingering in her thoughts (iv) causing numbness

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read the following sentence from the text: 'After all the obstacles are considered, if the burning conviction is still coursing through your veins, then go ahead and do something about it.' Here, the first conditional is used to talk about real or possible situations in the future. Complete the sentences using your own words (imperatives):

question_bank.stem: If you hear strange noises,_________________________.
If you care about the issue, _________________________.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read the following sentences from the text. Analyse the usage of 'could' and match the sentences with their functions given in the box below. (Past ability or purpose / Possibility / Past possibility or speculation / Unreal or hypothetical situation)

question_bank.stem: It could be in any field—sports, science, arts, business or design.
'I wish I could be this or the other,' they have never got beyond just wishful thinking.
They could have preferred to trade their dream for security.
I know too, of people who had wished to finish secondary school but they had to go out to work so that they could support their siblings through school.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Complete the following sentences using 'could' with the functions mentioned within brackets.

question_bank.stem: I wish ____________ without worrying about money. (unreal or hypothetical)
They ____________ the shorter route, but they didn't know about it. (past possibility or speculative)
When I was younger, I ____________ without stopping. (past ability)
She ____________ the library right now—she had mentioned it. (possibility)
____________ repeat the question? I didn't hear it clearly. (formal/polite request)

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Fill in the blanks with suitable grammatical forms of the words given in brackets. (Passage on experiential learning — 10 blanks)

question_bank.stem: Experiential learning is an approach... It 1. _______ (help) students gain practical knowledge...
Traditionally, students 2. _______ (learn) by listening to lectures...
In an experiential setting, students 3. _______ (work) on projects...
They 4. _______ (apply) what they have learned in class...
...a student studying science 5. _______ (conduct) experiments...
...while a business student 6. _______ (analyse) a case study...
After completing a task, learners 7. _______ (must/reflect) on what went well...
This reflection 8. _______ (help) them build a deeper understanding...
Educators believe that experiential learning 9. _______ (be) more effective...
...more schools and universities 10. _______ (incorporate) experiential learning...

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read the following sentence from the text and find out the meaning of the given '-scape' compound words (dictionary activity — mindscape, seascape, landscape, cityscape). [Part of Task I above; combined as one task object for the dictionary lookup component.]

listening.transcript_text: An announcement to students about a new set of vocational courses starting from the current academic year. The school will offer Graphic Design, Basic Coding, Web Application, Artificial Intelligence, Multimedia, Data Science, and Entrepreneurship Skills. All courses will be conducted by trained professionals at the school. Interested students from Grades 9 to 12 may collect the registration form from the school office starting Monday, 04 April; the last date for submission is Friday, 08 April. Seats are limited: each student can choose only one course, and preference will be given to early applicants. The announcement closes by encouraging students not to miss this opportunity to develop real-world skills beyond the textbook.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: You will listen to an announcement regarding vocational courses starting in school. As you listen, fill in the blanks with one to three exact words that you hear.

question_bank.stem: Vocational courses will begin in the month of _____.
The objective of the courses is to prepare you with ______ for future studies and jobs.
Offered courses include Graphic Design, ______,Web Application, and Entrepreneurship Skills, etc.
Classes will be conducted by ______ at school.
The application forms will be available at the ______.
Choice of course will be given to ______ applicants.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Work in groups of four. Each member of the group takes up one of the roles given below. Use the sentence prompts given under each role to present your role play. Roles: 1. The Dreamer (wants to be a trekking guide), 2. A Parent (worried about financial stability), 3. A Mentor or Teacher (offers balanced advice), 4. A Friend/Sibling (neutral or supportive voice).

question_bank.stem: The Dreamer: 'I know this might sound challenging, but…'; 'Ever since I was a child, I've dreamed of…'; 'I understand it's not a usual path, but I believe…'; 'It's an uphill journey, but I'm ready for it because…'
A Parent: 'I know you're passionate, but…'; 'I only want what's best for you, and…'; 'You can always pursue your dream later…'; 'What if it doesn't…?'
A Mentor or Teacher: 'Remember dreams need planning…'; 'Let's look at the long-term view…'; 'There are ways to combine your dream and…'; 'Are you ready to…?'

A Friend/Sibling: 'You've always been good at…don't give up now.'; 'I don't want to be a wet blanket, but what's your backup plan?'; 'If anyone can do this, it's you, so…'; 'If this dream makes you happy…'

tasks_verbatim.task_text: You are passionate about pursuing a course in designing in future. You come across a summer workshop being conducted by a reputed design institute. Write an email to the Director of the institute enquiring the details of the workshop and expressing your interest in joining it. Follow the guidelines: use formal language, avoid abbreviations, include header (sender, receiver, date, subject), message (introduction, seeking permission, conclusion), complementary close (Yours sincerely), name/designation/contact details.

Create a Vision Board for yourself. A vision board is a visual representation of your goals, dreams, and inspirations. Complete each of the following sections: 1. My Dreams, 2. I Want to Try, 3. Goals, 4. Places I Will Go, 5. Inspiration.

question_bank.stem: My Dreams — Think about what you wish for in the future. Write or draw it in the 'My dreams' section.
I Want to Try — List new things you want to experience this year.
Goals — Think about specific things you want to achieve.
Places I Will Go — Real or imaginary places you'd like to visit.
Inspiration — Who or what inspires you?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Great personalities in history were inspired by their parents. Read the examples of Chhatrapati Shivaji (mother Jijabai), Queen Madalasa, and Dr. APJ Abdul Kalam. Now, find out about such personalities from your village, town, city, or state and present their success stories in class.

question_bank.stem: Research example 1: Chhatrapati Shivaji — inspired by mother Jijabai's stories from the Ramayana and Mahabharata.
Research example 2: Queen Madalasa — guided her children toward spiritual liberation.
Research task: Find a local personality who turned challenges into success and present their story in class.
